response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with a long track record across web, embedded, and systems work, but the profile does not match this role's core requirements. There is no evidence of Kotlin or Spring experience, only minimal Java exposure, and no deep JVM expertise. His IDE-related experience (LabWindows/CVI components in C/C++) and developer-tooling work (D-language test runners, MCP generators) show genuine interest in tooling, but the JVM-ecosystem gap is too large for a role centered on enterprise Java/Kotlin tooling.
strengths: Genuine developer-tooling background: built test runners, assertion libraries, and contributed to an IDE product (NI LabWindows/CVI)
Long, varied engineering career with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Proven ability to work on complex, performance-sensitive systems (low-latency D services handling millions of requests/sec)
concerns: No demonstrable Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience, both core requirements
Java exposure is minimal and dated; primary stacks are TypeScript/Node.js and D
No recent work with enterprise JVM frameworks, build tools, or application servers

key_factors.factor: Complex-systems and performance engineering track record
